info.name: Adobe Commerce Optimizer – Categories API
info.description:
Postman collection for creating and managing categories via the Adobe Commerce Optimizer Data Ingestion REST API.

## Setup
Set the following Collection Variables before running any request:
- `tenantId` – from Commerce Cloud Manager → Instance Details
- `clientId` – from Adobe Developer Console
- `clientSecret` – from Adobe Developer Console
- `region` – e.g. `na1`
- `environment` – `sandbox` for non-production, leave empty for production

Run **"0. Get Access Token"** first. The token is saved automatically to `accessToken` via a test script.

exec: console.error('Failed to get access token:', JSON.stringify(json));

request.description: Generates an IMS access token using client credentials. The token is automatically saved to the `accessToken` collection variable via the Test script.
Creates a single root-level (level 1) category. The `slug` is the unique identifier and determines hierarchy. `parentSlug` is empty for root categories.
Creates a level 2 child category under the 'men' root. The `parentSlug` must reference an existing category slug.
Creates a level 3 (grandchild) category. Images are optional — omit for leaf categories that don't need a hero image.

Creates multiple categories in a single API call. The array can contain categories across different levels — the API processes all of them together. A successful response returns: {"status": "ACCEPTED", "acceptedCount": 4}
Creates categories under a separate `seasonal` family. Use distinct family names when your catalog has multiple independent category trees (e.g. main navigation vs. seasonal campaigns).

item.name: 6. Create Category with Full Rich Content

request.description: Creates a fully populated category with description, SEO metaTags, multiple images with different roles, and customRoles. Use this shape for rich category landing pages.
Assigns an existing product to one or more categories by referencing their slugs in the `categories` array. A product can belong to multiple categories across different families.
